original_image_tags: A faded yellowed paper document with hole punches at the top. Several handwritten initials and notes are visible at the bottom, along with stamps and a redacted black bar.
A stamp on the right side reads "JUN 13 6 12 PM RECEIVED RECORDS DIV U.S. DEPT OF JUSTICE FBI".
A red stamp at the bottom left reads "MAILED B AUG 14 1952 COMM - FBI".
A faint stamp at the very bottom left reads "53 AUG 20 1952".
A list of names is printed in the bottom left margin: "Tolson", "Ladd", "Nichols", "Belmont", "Clegg", "Glavin", "Harbo", "Rosen", "Tracy", "Laughlin", "Mohr", "Tele. Rm.", "Holloman", "Candy".

elements.description: Typed letter dated August 13, 1952, addressed to Mr. W. H. Jennings regarding a letter about flying saucers, with a note for Detroit and a vertical stamp reading 'JUN 13 6:12 PM RECEIVED RECORDS U.S. DEPT. OF STATE F.B.I.' on the right side.

judge.reason: Description A details multiple stamps with specific dates, text, and a list of names, while Description B provides only one stamp with different text ('U.S. DEPT. OF STATE' vs 'DEPT OF JUSTICE') and omits all other described visual elements.
